name: Aalo
tagline: Siang valley base town — the gateway to Mechuka and the only reliable logistical stop in West Siang.
new_note: Arunachal's West Siang HQ — Protected Area Permit + organised operator (Abor Country Travels) is the only format. Village homestays vetted via operator.

name: Achabal
tagline: Queen of Kashmir's springs — Mughal terraced garden with natural cascading water channels and chinar shade.
new_note: Mughal gardens near Anantnag — Kashmir pilgrim + tourist flow in summer (Jul-Sep). Don't overnight; day-trip from Srinagar.

name: Adilabad
tagline: Kuntala Falls, tiger reserve, and Gond tribal culture — Telangana's wild north
new_note: Telangana's northernmost district + Kawal sanctuary — no tourist infrastructure. Skip solo; organised researcher-traveller format only.

name: Agartala
permit_required: No permit required for Indian or foreign nationals.
tagline: Ujjayanta Palace, Neermahal water palace, and India's most underrated state capital on the Bangladesh border.
new_note: Tripura capital — Ujjayanta Palace + Neermahal are tourist-regular, but curfew-era caution in outlying areas persists. Daylight sightseeing, organised tours.

name: Agatti Island
permit_required: MANDATORY: Entry Permit required. Apply through Lakshadweep tourism or SPORTS.
tagline: Gateway to Lakshadweep — the only island with an airport and a stunning coral reef runway approach
new_note: Lakshadweep gateway island — airport + dive base, Agatti Island Beach Resort is the pick. Permit-regulated = safe.

name: Agonda
tagline: Goa's quietest beach — turtle nesting site, yoga retreats, and nothing else (that's the point)
new_note: Quiet South Goa beach, overwhelmingly solo-female backpacker clientele. Turtle-nesting + yoga-retreat zone. Fatrade Beach stretch is the safest in all of Goa.

name: Agra
tagline: The Taj Mahal is worth every cliche ever written about it — and Mehtab Bagh at sunset, when the crowds thin, is when it stops being a monument and starts being magic.
new_note: Taj tourism is safe; everything else in Agra has scam + harassment documented. Stay near Taj East/South Gate, organised tour for Fatehpur Sikri.

name: Ahmedabad
tagline: India's first UNESCO Heritage City — Sabarmati Ashram, stepwells, and vibrant old city pols
new_note: Alcohol-prohibition + BRTS + metro — Gujarat's strongest female-safety metro. SG Road, Navrangpura are night-safe. Old city Pol-walk groups recommended.

name: Ahobilam
tagline: Nine Narasimha temples hidden in one forest gorge — globally unique
new_note: AP Nava Narasimha circuit — 9 shrines + trek. Male-dominated pilgrim flow, thin infrastructure. Organised yatra-bus from Kurnool only; skip solo.

name: Aihole
tagline: Cradle of Indian rock architecture — 120+ temples where Chalukya architects experimented
new_note: Chalukya temple complex + Badami/Pattadakal circuit — ASI day-trip from Badami. No overnight; stay in Badami (KSTDC Mayura).

name: Aizawl
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) required for all Indian citizens. Apply online at mizoram.nic.in — free. Foreigners need Restricted Area Permit.
tagline: A city built on ridges — churches on every hill, the cleanest state, and zero begging.
new_note: Mizoram capital — dry-state + strong Christian community = low street harassment. Mizo women routinely solo-travel. Regency + Clover are safe.

name: Ajanta Caves
tagline: UNESCO masterpiece — 2,000-year-old Buddhist paintings that survived in horseshoe rock
new_note: UNESCO Buddhist cave-paintings near Aurangabad — ASI-staffed, MTDC bus + authorised guides. Daytime tour from Aurangabad; return by evening, don't overnight at Fardapur.

name: Ajmer
tagline: One of India most important Sufi shrines — and the gateway everyone rushes through to reach Pushkar.
new_note: Dargah Sharif urs crowd is hugely mixed but pickpocketing + touts target women. Stay near station, not Dargah-adjacent lanes. Day-trip from Pushkar works better.

name: Alampur
tagline: Navabrahma — nine 7th-century Shiva temples where two rivers meet

name: Alchi
tagline: A 1000-year-old Buddhist art gallery hidden in a tiny village — the oldest surviving murals in Ladakh, painted when the Chola dynasty ruled South India.
new_note: 11th-century monastery in Ladakh — day-trip from Leh, monastery + homestay via Alchi Kitchen are safe. Winter (Nov-Mar) is closed.

name: Alibaug
tagline: Mumbai's beach backyard — forts, ferries, and the Konkan coast at your doorstep
new_note: Mumbai weekend coast — upmarket villa rentals (Escape, Saffronstays) are gated + vetted. Avoid Nagaon beach stag-group weekend zone.

name: Alleppey (Alappuzha)
tagline: Venice of the East — 900km of canals, houseboats, and the Nehru Trophy Boat Race
new_note: Kerala backwater houseboat hub — Kerala Tourism-regulated operators only (check ATDC registration). Solo female houseboat well-established, overnight + cook + crew included.

name: Almora
tagline: The Kumaoni heritage town where Kasar Devi cosmic energy attracted Swami Vivekananda, D.H. Lawrence, and Timothy Leary before Instagram discovered it.
new_note: Kumaon heritage town — Kasar Devi area is Western-traveller-friendly. Chitai + Golu temples daytime. Stay in Kasar Devi or town-centre hotels.

name: Alwar
tagline: Bhangarh ghost fort, Sariska tiger reserve, and a city palace that rivals Jaipur — all in India most ignored Rajasthan district.
new_note: Rajasthan's Sariska-base city — Bala Qila + City Palace daytime fine. Stay at RTDC Meenal or Alwar Bagh, not station-area lodges. Sariska day-trip via FD jeep.

name: Amaravati
tagline: Cradle of Mahayana Buddhism — where the Great Stupa stood 2,200 years ago
new_note: AP heritage town + proposed new capital — daytime tourism thin, evenings empty. Day-trip from Vijayawada.

name: Ambaji
tagline: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas — sacred hilltop temple near the Rajasthan border
new_note: Shakti-peeth on Gujarat-Rajasthan border; temple trust runs women's dorms + security. Bhadrapada Poornima week = 5 million pilgrims, skip that window.

name: Amboli
tagline: Maharashtra's southernmost hill station — Sahyadri waterfalls, king cobras, and monsoon magic
new_note: Maharashtra-Karnataka border Sahyadri hill — MTDC resort + Whistling Woods. Monsoon waterfall season (Jul-Sep) is the crowd; off-season thin overnight solo.

name: Amritsar
tagline: The Golden Temple doesn't just feed your soul — it literally feeds 100,000 people a day, for free, regardless of who you are.
new_note: Golden Temple serves 100K free meals daily; women volunteers always visible, 24/7 langar access. Safest religious city in India for solo women.

name: Anamalai Tiger Reserve (Topslip)
tagline: Topslip — lion-tailed macaques, bamboo rafting, and the Anamalai Hills
new_note: Valparai + Top Slip tiger reserve — organised TN Forest Dept safari. Stay in Valparai estate bungalows (Briar Tea, Sinnadorai), not Topslip only.

name: Anandpur Sahib
tagline: Virasat-e-Khalsa museum alone is worth the trip — architecture that rivals the Guggenheim, telling the story of Sikh heritage.
new_note: Sikh holy town — gurdwara langar tradition + Holla Mohalla festival. Safe year-round; Takht complex has women's accommodation.

name: Ananthagiri Hills
tagline: Hyderabad's nearest hill escape — 79km to misty forests and ancient temples
new_note: Telangana coffee-hills near Hyderabad — thin tourist infrastructure, Haritha Hill Resort is the safe pick. Day-trip or one-night.

name: Anini
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) MANDATORY for all Indian citizens. Apply online via Arunachal Pradesh eILP portal or at Guwahati/Tezpur facilitation centers.
tagline: Most remote district HQ in India — Dibang Valley with red pandas and Mehao Wildlife Sanctuary
new_note: Dibang Valley's remote town — military + tribal permit zone, no civilian tourist infrastructure. Skip solo, organised researchers only.

name: Anjuna
tagline: Where the hippie trail ends — flea markets, trance parties, and Chapora Fort sunsets
new_note: Party strip — fine at brunch cafes, avoid the late-night hippie-flea market walk alone. Stay in Vagator-side guesthouses, not the rave-shack stretch.

name: Araku Valley
tagline: Coffee country in the Eastern Ghats — Borra Caves, tribal culture, and a toy train
new_note: Tribal valley + coffee + train journey from Vizag — organised APTDC/IRCTC package is the female-safe way. Skip solo overnight in villages.

name: Arambol
tagline: Goa's last hippie beach — drum circles, sweet lake, paragliding, and zero pretension
new_note: Long-stay Russian + Israeli + European female backpacker hub. Drum-circle culture + yoga retreats. Stay near Sweet Lake, not the far-north stretch.

name: Athirapally Falls
tagline: Niagara of India — India's largest waterfall by volume flow
new_note: Kerala's biggest waterfall + monsoon-Sep peak — Rainforest Resort + KTDC are the safe stays. Day-trip from Kochi/Thrissur regular.

name: Auli
tagline: India's best (and basically only real) ski slope, with a front-row view of Nanda Devi.
new_note: Ski resort + GMVN hotels are safe; ropeway + ski-school has vetted instructors. Solo winter drive iffy, prefer Joshimath base.

name: Aurangabad
tagline: Gateway to UNESCO caves — Bibi Ka Maqbara, Paithan silks, and Mughlai-Deccani food
new_note: Ellora + Ajanta base — MTDC Holiday Resort + Ambassador are solo-safe. City outside hotels has touts; use pre-paid auto. Bibi-Ka-Maqbara daytime.

name: Auroville
tagline: 50-year intentional community — Matrimandir, organic farms, and 3,300 residents from 60 nations
new_note: International experimental-community near Pondicherry — inherently female-collaborative. Matrimandir access + community kitchens + all-female dorms routine.

name: Ayodhya
tagline: The newly built Ram Mandir has turned a 500-year-old dispute into India biggest pilgrimage boom.
new_note: New temple infrastructure post-2024, but tourism crowd density is unfamiliar to the town. Stick to organised tours, day-trip from Lucknow.

name: Badami
tagline: Chalukya cave temples carved from red sandstone cliffs — India's earliest Hindu rock architecture
new_note: Chalukyan cave-temples + Agastya lake — KSTDC Mayura + Heritage Resort are female-safe. Daytime climb, evening cave-tour in group.

name: Badrinath
tagline: One of the four Char Dham — a 1,200-year-old Vishnu temple at 3,133m that closes for six months because even the gods leave for winter.

name: Bandhavgarh National Park
permit_required: none
tagline: India's highest tiger density — your best odds of spotting a tiger
new_note: Tiger safari + MPTDC White Tiger Forest Lodge — organised, solo-women-routine. Stay in Tala gate hotels (MPTDC, Tigergarh), not roadside lodges.

name: Bandipur National Park
tagline: Nilgiri Biosphere tiger reserve — elephants, leopards, and India's most scenic safari road
new_note: JLR Bandipur + Mysore-adjacent — organised jeep/bus safari. Solo overnight at JLR fine; roadside private lodges mixed.

name: Bangaram Island
permit_required: Entry permit required. Only island open to foreign nationals.
tagline: India's Maldives — an uninhabited resort island with the clearest water in the subcontinent
new_note: Lakshadweep resort-only island — CGH Earth property = solo-safe, gated. Water-scarce infrastructure = expensive but female-routine.

name: Bangus Valley
tagline: A permit-required frontier valley near the LoC that rewards the bureaucratic effort with meadows nobody else is standing in.
new_note: Kashmir's unmarked valley trek — Kupwara-district permit-zone. Organised operator (JK Tourism + Kupwara DTO) only; solo not feasible.

name: Baratang Island
permit_required: Indian nationals: none. Foreigners: need additional permit from Deputy Commissioner office
tagline: Limestone caves, mud volcanoes, and mangrove creek boat rides through Jarawa territory
new_note: Andaman's limestone caves + mud-volcano + Jarawa-reserve pass — organised tour from Port Blair only (security convoy). Day-trip; no solo overnight.

name: Baripada
tagline: Chhau dance birthplace — UNESCO Intangible Heritage, Simlipal gateway, tribal Mayurbhanj
new_note: Odisha tribal district HQ + Simlipal gateway — no solo traveller infrastructure, organised operator required. Skip unless going Simlipal with a registered lodge.

name: Barmer
tagline: The Rajasthan even Rajasthanis skip — hand-block printing villages, desert sand dunes without the Jaisalmer tourist machinery.
new_note: Rajasthan's westernmost district — heritage haveli + textile. Garh Jaisalmer + RTDC. Day-trip combine with Jaisalmer preferred.

name: Barnawapara
permit_required: false
tagline: Zero-crowd central Indian wildlife sanctuary
new_note: Chhattisgarh reserve — thin tourist flow, Naxal-adjacent belt. Organised Jungle Camp via CTDC only. Skip solo.

name: Barot Valley
tagline: The valley that Google Maps forgot — no ATM, no hospital, no Mall Road, just the Uhl river and you.
new_note: Mandi's offbeat river valley — HPTDC rest house + Barot homestay (Ani Trekkers). Thin for solo overnight but doable with pre-booking.

name: Barren Island
permit_required: Forest department permit required — apply at Port Blair forest office (₹500)
tagline: India's only active volcano — view from boat only, no landing allowed
new_note: Only active volcano in South Asia — no landing, distant-view boat only. Not a tourist visit in any solo format.

name: Basara
tagline: One of only two Saraswathi temples in India — where children begin their education
new_note: Saraswati temple pilgrimage during Vasant Panchami brings families; off-season the town has minimal infrastructure. Day-trip from Nizamabad.

name: Bekal
tagline: Kerala's largest fort overlooking the Arabian Sea
new_note: Fort + beach combo in Kasaragod — Vivanta Bekal + Lalit Resort are the luxury options, gated + safe. Beach is family-tourist.

name: Belum Caves
tagline: India's 2nd longest cave system — 3.2km of stalactites deep underground
new_note: AP's underground cave system near Gandikota — APTDC Haritha Cottages + daytime guided tour. Same-day return or overnight combined with Gandikota.

name: Belur
tagline: UNESCO Hoysala temple — Chennakesava's soapstone carvings are the most intricate in India
new_note: Hoysala temple complex — ASI + women's weaving SHGs around the site. Day-trip from Hassan or Bangalore, pair with Halebidu.

name: Bengaluru
tagline: India's Silicon Valley — craft beer capital, garden city, and the startup engine of a billion people
new_note: Metro + Namma Yatri/Ola women-friendly. Indiranagar, Koramangala, Church Street are night-safe; outer ORR stretches are iffy solo after 11pm.

name: Beypore
tagline: 1,000-year-old hand-built wooden ships — the last Uru shipyard on Earth
new_note: Kerala's traditional-boat-building port near Kozhikode — day-trip from Kozhikode, cultural tourism heavy with families.

name: Bhaderwah
tagline: The Mini Kashmir that nobody from outside J&K has heard of — meadows, deodar forests, and zero tourists.
new_note: Jammu's meadow-town — Patnitop–Bhaderwah highway, JKTDC Hut + Bhaderwah Hotel. Thin solo-female flow; organised camping format recommended.

name: Bhadrachalam
tagline: Rama Temple on the Godavari — 100,000 pilgrims flood in on Vaikuntha Ekadasi
new_note: Godavari Ram temple — peaceful pilgrim town, but the NH connections have AP-TG border checkpoint waits. Same-day return.

name: Bhalukpong
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) MANDATORY for all Indian citizens. Apply online via Arunachal Pradesh eILP portal or at Guwahati/Tezpur facilitation centers.
tagline: Pakhui Tiger Reserve gateway on the Kameng River — first stop in Arunachal from Assam
new_note: Arunachal gateway + Pakke TR entry — permit required, organised operator (Assam-based). Day-trip from Tezpur; no solo-female overnight format.

name: Bhandardara
tagline: Arthur Lake, fireflies in May, and Kalsubai — Maharashtra's rooftop
new_note: Maharashtra's Sahyadri lake + Kalsubai + monsoon firefly week (May). MTDC resort + Dreamland are safe. Organised firefly-week camps women-routine.

name: Bharatpur
tagline: Keoladeo Ghana UNESCO bird sanctuary — 350+ species including Siberian cranes, 55km from the Taj.
new_note: Keoladeo bird sanctuary — RTDC Bharatpur Forest Lodge + Bagh ka Bagh + Laxmi Vilas Palace. Cycle/rickshaw inside the park organised. Solo-women routine.

name: Bhavnagar
tagline: Gateway to Palitana — royal city with Takhteshwar Temple overlooking the Gulf of Cambay
new_note: Gujarat tier-2 city — alcohol-prohibition means street safety higher than average; transit risk around the bus stand. Blackbuck park day-trip is the draw.

name: Bhimashankar
tagline: One of 12 Jyotirlingas — Sahyadri temple, giant Indian squirrel sanctuary, and monsoon treks
new_note: Jyotirlinga inside a wildlife sanctuary — the trek-path is fine daytime, but overnight stays are dodgy homestays. Day-trip from Pune/Karjat.

name: Bhimbetka Rock Shelters
tagline: UNESCO World Heritage 2003 — 30,000-year-old rock paintings, the oldest evidence of human life on the Indian subcontinent.
new_note: UNESCO rock-art day-trip from Bhopal — ASI-staffed, safe. Stay in Bhopal, not Raisen. Combine with Sanchi same day.

name: Bhimtal
tagline: The quiet cousin who got the better genes — Bhimtal lake without the Nainital crowds, an island in the middle, and actual birdsong.
new_note: Quieter than Nainital — KMVN Tourist Rest House + Lake Resort are female-safe. Island cafe daytime; town outside the lake is dead.

name: Bhitarkanika
tagline: India's 2nd largest mangrove — 1,826 saltwater crocs, 18 albino, king cobras

name: Bhongir
tagline: Egg-shaped monolithic fort — a 10th-century geological freak you can climb
new_note: Rock fort near Hyderabad — daytime climb + tourism. Day-trip from Hyderabad, return by evening; no overnight option for solo women.

name: Bhopal
permit_required: false
tagline: City of Lakes
new_note: MP capital — Upper + Lower Lake + Bhimbetka day-trip. Stay near New Market + MP Nagar, not old city. Metro + Bhopal-Ola network reliable.

name: Bhubaneswar
tagline: Temple City — 700+ temples spanning 2,000 years of continuous worship

name: Bidar
tagline: Bahmani dynasty capital — massive fort, Bidriware metalcraft, and Guru Nanak's sacred spring
new_note: Bahmani heritage — fort + Mahmud Gawan madrasa. Daytime tourist spot, thin infrastructure. Day-trip from Kalaburagi or Hyderabad.

name: Bijapur (Vijayapura)
tagline: Gol Gumbaz — the world's second-largest unsupported dome, and the Adil Shahi dynasty's glory
new_note: Gol Gumbaz + Ibrahim Rauza are safe daytime; Bijapur town outside monuments is sparse. Day-trip from Kalaburagi, don't overnight.

name: Bikaner
tagline: Junagarh Fort that was never conquered, a temple full of rats, and a government camel research farm — Bikaner does not do normal.
new_note: Rajasthan tourism with Camel Research Farm + Junagarh Fort — heritage havelis (Laxmi Niwas) are female-staffed. Karni Mata temple day-trip is fine in a group.

name: Binsar
tagline: 300km Himalayan panorama from inside an oak forest sanctuary — and nobody around.
new_note: Kumaon wildlife sanctuary + Jageshwar — thin stays (KMVN rest house + Binsar Forest Retreat are the picks). Solo drive from Almora safer than bus.

name: Bir Billing
tagline: India paragliding capital — Tibetan colony and cafe scene mean you stay even if wind does not cooperate.
new_note: Paragliding + Himachal Tibetan-settlement + monastery — Tibetan-run guesthouses (Zostel Bir, Colonel's Resort) are safe. Tandem pilots are certified (registered operators only).

name: Bodh Gaya
permit_required: false
tagline: Where Siddhartha became Buddha
new_note: Buddhist tourism brings female solo travellers from Japan, Sri Lanka, the West. Stay near Mahabodhi, avoid the lanes after 9pm. Police presence during pilgrimage season.

name: Bomdila
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) MANDATORY for all Indian citizens. Apply online via Arunachal Pradesh eILP portal or at Guwahati/Tezpur facilitation centers.
tagline: Gateway to Tawang with Tibetan monasteries, apple orchards, and Sela Pass views
new_note: Arunachal gateway to Tawang — PAP required, organised tour + Apar Retreat hotel. Day-waypoint not a solo-destination.

name: Bundi
tagline: The Rajasthan that tourism forgot — 50 stepwells, a crumbling fort, painted havelis, and zero selfie sticks.
new_note: Underrated Rajasthan stop — fewer touts than Jaipur, haveli homestays (Bundi Haveli, Hadee Rani) are women-owned. Safer than Bundi-proper station area.

name: Calangute-Baga
tagline: Goa's busiest beach strip — water sports by day, clubs by night, shacks 24/7
new_note: Loudest Goa strip — charter-tourist males + sunbed harassment + night-club bouncer risk. Skip entirely unless part of an organised family trip.

name: Chail
tagline: World highest cricket ground, a palace hotel, and the hill station Shimla locals escape TO.
new_note: HPTDC palace hotel is the destination — gated, female-safe. Don't rely on local homestays without prior booking.

name: Chakrata
tagline: A cantonment town the army forgot to tell tourists about — Tiger Falls, Deoban's ancient forests, and zero commercial tourism.
new_note: Restricted cantonment zone + hill — foreigner permit required. GMVN rest house + Snow Peak resort are safe. Off-beat = thin solo-female peers.

name: Chamba
tagline: 1000-year-old Lakshmi Narayan temples, the Chaugan meadow, and Himachali culture untouched by tourism.
new_note: Himachal's old kingdom town — HPTDC Hotel Iravati is the safe pick. Chaugan ground evening crowds family-regular. Thin for solo women overnight outside HPTDC.

name: Champaner-Pavagadh
tagline: UNESCO archaeological park — mosques, temples, and fortifications in pristine condition
new_note: UNESCO site + Mahakali temple — ASI-managed, women pilgrims during Navratri. Day-trip from Vadodara only, town has no infrastructure.

name: Champawat
tagline: Where the Chand dynasty ruled Kumaon for 800 years — temples, fort ruins, and zero tourists.
new_note: Kumaon pilgrim town, old temples, quiet. Thin tourist economy = limited female-friendly homestays. Day-trip from Pithoragarh or Almora.

name: Champhai
permit_required: ILP required for Mizoram.
tagline: Rice bowl of Mizoram — Indo-Myanmar border panorama, Rih Dil lake (believed to be gateway to the afterworld in Mizo mythology), Murlen National Park
new_note: Mizoram-Myanmar-border rice-bowl town — sparse tourist infrastructure, mostly Mizoram Tourism circuit-house. Skip solo.

name: Chandigarh
tagline: India best-planned city — Le Corbusier dream that actually works, with a rock garden built from literal garbage.
new_note: Planned city = wide roads, good policing, women-safe night transit. Sector 17 + 22 are reliable. Rock Garden + Sukhna Lake daytime.

name: Chandipur
tagline: The disappearing sea — water recedes 5km every day, exposing the ocean floor
new_note: Odisha disappearing-sea beach — daytime phenomenon-tourism. OTDC Panthanivas is the stay. No nightlife, no night-walking.

name: Chandratal
tagline: Crescent-shaped lake at 4,300m — reachable only in summer, camped beside on borrowed time.
new_note: Moon Lake at 4,300m — camp-only site, only via organised Parasol/Moonlake Camps. Altitude + no network = group-only. Don't attempt solo.

name: Charaideo Moidams
tagline: India's 43rd UNESCO World Heritage Site — the Pyramids of Assam, royal burial mounds of 600 years of Ahom Dynasty rule.
new_note: Ahom burial mounds UNESCO site — remote Assam, no tourist infrastructure. Organised tour from Jorhat only, no solo option.

name: Chaukori
tagline: Tea gardens at 2010m with Panchachuli peaks as backdrop — the view Kausani gets credit for, but Chaukori does better.
new_note: Kumaon tea-garden hill — KMVN rest house + Chaukori Retreat are the safe stays. Panchachuli view, quiet, thin solo flow.

name: Chennai
tagline: Marina Beach, Fort St. George, Carnatic music — South India's cultural capital
new_note: MRTS + Chennai Metro + share-auto culture makes solo transit safe. T. Nagar + Mylapore temple lanes are family-dense even after 10pm. Avoid the Marina post-sunset.

name: Cherai Beach
tagline: Dolphins, Chinese fishing nets, and the estuary where Periyar meets the sea
new_note: Kochi's quiet beach suburb — Les 3 Elephants boutique is female-owned. Short taxi from Kochi, doable as a solo overnight.

name: Cherrapunji (Sohra)
permit_required: No permit required for Indian or foreign nationals.
tagline: Where clouds live — living root bridges, Nohkalikai Falls, and one of the wettest places on Earth.
new_note: Rainfall-record town — organised day-tour from Shillong covers Nohkalikai + Double-decker bridge. Don't overnight solo in outskirts.

name: Chettinad
tagline: India's spiciest cuisine and 10,000 palatial mansions with Burma teak and Italian marble
new_note: Heritage-mansion tourism — Karaikudi + Kanadukathan palaces are female-guided, cooking classes popular. Visalam, Chidambara Vilas are female-owned.

name: Chidambaram
tagline: Nataraja Temple — Shiva as Cosmic Dancer, the Pancha Bhuta Stala of Space
new_note: Nataraja temple traffic keeps the town active. Stay near the temple — the outskirts have no night transport.

name: Chidiya Tapu
permit_required: Indian nationals: none. Foreigners: standard permit
tagline: Bird Island — Andaman's best sunset point with mangrove walks, forest treks, and 50+ bird species
new_note: Port Blair's sunset-beach + birding — day-trip via A&N Tourism. No overnight solo option; return to Port Blair.

name: Chikmagalur
tagline: Where Indian coffee was born — Mullayanagiri peak, Baba Budangiri, and the best plantation stays
new_note: Coffee estate + Mullayanagiri — plantation-stays (Trivik, Java Rain) are gated + safe. Don't trust roadside homestays without reviews.

name: Chilika Lake
tagline: Asia's largest brackish lagoon — Irrawaddy dolphins, million migratory birds, Nalabana Island
new_note: Odisha's brackish lagoon + dolphin + Kalijai — OTDC Panthaniwas Barkul + Rambha are safe. Boat safari organised; Mangalajodi bird-village vetted homestays.

name: Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Kerala's driest sanctuary — Grizzled Giant Squirrel and Thoovanam Falls
new_note: Kerala-TN border wildlife — permit + guide required. Day-trip from Munnar; don't overnight in the forest-dept hut unless with a group.

name: Chitkul
tagline: The last inhabited village before the Tibet border — where India ends and the mountains begin.
new_note: India's last Hindustan-Tibet-Highway village before Tibet — Kinnaur's 3,450m Sangla end. HPTDC Kinner Kailash + Banjara camps. Weekend crowd + solo travellers present.

name: Chitradurga
tagline: Seven concentric walls, 1,500 acres — the fort that withstood every invader until Hyder Ali
new_note: Karnataka fort town — daytime fort climb safe, KSRTC bus from Hospet works. Stay in KSTDC, not private lodges. Don't overnight in fort stretch.

name: Chitrakoot
tagline: Where Ram spent 11 of his 14 years of exile — the most important Ramayana site outside Ayodhya.
new_note: Ram-Sita pilgrim circuit split across MP/UP — MP side (Ramghat) is safer than UP side. Dandi-Mauni temple area thins out fast after 7pm.

name: Chittorgarh
tagline: India's largest fort — 700 acres of Rajput defiance, Padmini's palace, and the Tower of Victory.
new_note: Massive fort + Meera temple — ASI-staffed, tourist-regular. Stay in cantonment area, not the old town lanes. Padmavati legend draws women travellers.

name: Chopta
tagline: Mini Switzerland of Uttarakhand — rhododendron meadows, Himalayan views, and the easiest gateway to a 4000m summit.
new_note: Mini-Switzerland + Tungnath trek base — camp-style stays only, pre-booked (Brahmakamal Camps, Monal). Don't rely on roadside dhaba-stays.

name: Chorao & Divar Islands
tagline: Goa's forgotten islands — bird sanctuary, Portuguese mansions, and zero tourists

name: Coimbatore
tagline: Adiyogi statue (112ft Guinness record) and the gateway to the Nilgiris
new_note: TN's manufacturing hub + Ooty gateway — Race Course area + RS Puram are night-safe. Metro + Ola reliable. Day-stop en route Ooty/Coonoor.

name: Colva & Benaulim
tagline: Where Goans actually go to the beach — long quiet sand, local fish restaurants, zero hype
new_note: South Goa family beaches — Portuguese-villa homestays are female-run, charter-tourist mix dilutes party scene. Safer than Calangute, quieter than Palolem.

name: Coonoor
tagline: Nilgiri tea capital — quieter than Ooty, on the UNESCO toy train route
new_note: Nilgiri quieter twin of Ooty — Tea-garden tourism, Wellington cantonment-quiet. KTDC Hampton + La Maison are safe.

name: Coorg (Kodagu)
tagline: India's coffee capital — misty plantations, Abbey Falls, and the warrior culture of Kodagu

name: Jim Corbett National Park
tagline: India oldest national park and the best place to see a wild tiger without Rajasthan crowds.
new_note: Organised safari + CTH/Dhikala lodges are regulated. Solo-women on jeep safari standard since 1990s. Stay inside the core-zone FRH only via pre-booking.

name: Courtallam (Kutralam)
tagline: Spa of South India — 9 herb-infused waterfalls with natural healing properties
new_note: TN's monsoon-waterfall town — Nine Falls circuit, TTDC + private lodges. June-Sep is the high season when crowd = safer; off-season dead.

name: Cuttack
tagline: Silver City — 1,000-year-old filigree craft capital and Odisha's cultural heart
new_note: Odisha's old capital — Barabati fort + silver filigree workshops. Safer than Bhubaneswar core, but thin hotels. Day-trip combine with Bhubaneswar.

name: Dakor
tagline: Ranchhodrai Temple — "Dwarka of the East" for millions of Krishna devotees
new_note: Ranchhodrai temple near Ahmedabad — women's trust-run stays, family-heavy crowd. Day-trip 90 mins from Ahmedabad.

name: Dalhousie
tagline: Colonial hill station with Scottish churches, Khajjiar meadow (Mini Switzerland), and cedar-lined walks.
new_note: Himachal tier-2 hill — quieter than Shimla, thinner transport after dark. Stay at HPTDC Geetanjali or near Gandhi Chowk only.

name: Daman
tagline: Portuguese forts, serene beaches, and Goan-flavored heritage on Gujarat's coast
new_note: Gujarat-adjacent prohibition-exempt zone = male alcohol tourism heavy. Devka + Jampore beach stretches feel less female-safe than Diu.

name: Dambuk
tagline: Orange-growing Dibang valley village that hosts India's most remote music festival each December.
new_note: Arunachal's Orange Festival (Dec) venue — organised festival operator is the safest + only format. Off-festival, no infrastructure.

name: Takht Sri Damdama Sahib
tagline: One of only 5 Sikh Takhts — where Guru Gobind Singh compiled the final Guru Granth Sahib in 1706.
new_note: Guru Gobind Singh's Takht in Bathinda district — gurdwara langar hospitality + Punjab's strong Sikh female-safety norm. Safe solo stay.

name: Dandeli
tagline: White water rafting, crocodiles, and 834 sq km of Sahyadri wilderness
new_note: Karnataka adventure + wildlife — organised rafting (Kali River Camp, Hornbill River Resort). Not a solo-female intro; group format.

name: Daringbadi
tagline: "Kashmir of Odisha" — pine forests, coffee gardens, and Kutia Kondh tribal life
new_note: Odisha's 'Kashmir' hill — OTDC Panthaniwas is the safe pick. Tribal-belt context = organised tour operator recommended.

name: Darjeeling
tagline: The Queen of Hills — toy train, tea gardens, and Kanchenjunga sunrise. Colonial-era charm meets Tibetan-Nepali street culture.
new_note: Toy-train tourism + Bengali family flow + Tibetan monastic presence = female-safe. Chowrasta + Mall Road are night-fine. Tiger Hill sunrise group tours.

name: Daulatabad Fort
tagline: The impregnable hilltop fortress — moats, dark tunnels, and a 200m vertical climb

name: Dawki
permit_required: No permit required for Indian or foreign nationals.
tagline: Crystal-clear Umngot River — where boats appear to float on glass at the India-Bangladesh border.
new_note: Meghalaya-Bangladesh border Umngot clear-river — organised day-trip from Shillong or overnight at Shnongpdeng + Umngot campsites (organised operator).

name: Deeg
tagline: A water palace where 900 fountains once fired simultaneously — Rajasthan's most underrated monsoon palace.
new_note: Bharatpur heritage — Jal Mahal is ASI-managed. Day-trip from Bharatpur combined with Keoladeo. Don't overnight.

name: Delhi
tagline: Not a destination — a gateway. But if you give it 2 days, it'll surprise you with Mughal tombs, street food, and a chaos that becomes addictive.
new_note: Metro is reliable (ladies coach + CCTV). South Delhi (Hauz Khas, Saket, Lodi) is night-safer than Old Delhi or transit hubs. Avoid auto-rickshaw at 2am; use Uber/Ola.

name: Deoghar
permit_required: false
tagline: Baidyanath Jyotirlinga — one of 12 sacred Shiva temples
new_note: Baidyanath Jyotirlinga + Shravan Mela flood — solo women should avoid July-August. Rest of year safe but thin stay infrastructure.

name: Deomali
tagline: Odisha's highest peak at 1,672m — gateway to Dongria Kondh tribal country
new_note: Arunachal's remote peak + tribal belt — no established trek infrastructure, militant-insurgency-adjacent. Skip solo absolutely.

name: Devprayag
tagline: Where Alaknanda meets Bhagirathi and the Ganges officially begins.
new_note: Ganga-Alaknanda sangam — clean pilgrim town, GMVN rest house safe. Daytime confluence + Raghunath temple. Safer than Rishikesh for a quiet stay.

name: Dhanaulti
tagline: The quiet alternative to Mussoorie — 24km further, 90% fewer tourists.

name: Dhanushkodi
tagline: India's only ghost town — 1964 cyclone ruins at the absolute tip of India
new_note: Ghost-town on Rameswaram's tip — day-tour by jeep only, no overnight (no electricity). Tourist-safe in organised group.

name: Dharamshala
tagline: Cricket stadium with the best view in world sport, Tibetan government-in-exile, and gateway to the Dhauladhar range.
new_note: Upper (McLeodganj) is safer than lower town. Stay in McLeodganj or Dharamkot, not lower Kotwali Bazaar area.

name: Dharmasthala
tagline: The temple that feeds 30,000 people free every day — Dharmasthala's annadana tradition
new_note: Jain-administered Shiva temple with women-only dorms. Tulunadu cultural norm makes solo women travellers routine, not remarkable.

name: Dhauli
tagline: Where Ashoka's conscience awoke — Kalinga War site, rock edicts, peace pagoda
new_note: Ashoka-edict site + Shanti Stupa near Bhubaneswar — daytime tour-safe, OTDC included in city tour. Day-trip from Bhubaneswar; no overnight.

name: Dholavira
tagline: UNESCO Harappan city — one of the 5 largest Indus Valley settlements
new_note: Kutch's UNESCO Harappan site — KPWD heritage cottage + organised Rann Utsav routes only. Don't solo-drive without prior stays booked.

name: Buddhist Diamond Triangle
tagline: Lalitgiri + Ratnagiri + Udayagiri — Buddhist ruins rivaling Ajanta, UNESCO tentative

name: Diglipur
permit_required: Indian nationals: none. Foreigners: need permit
tagline: Saddle Peak (highest point in Andaman), turtle nesting beaches, and the stunning Ross & Smith twin islands
new_note: North Andaman end-town — Ross & Smith islands + turtle beach. Sparse infrastructure, Turtle Resort is the pick. Ferry timings dictate plans.

name: Dirang
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) MANDATORY for all Indian citizens. Apply online via Arunachal Pradesh eILP portal or at Guwahati/Tezpur facilitation centers.
tagline: Hot springs village with kiwi orchards, Sangti Valley cranes, and a 500-year-old dzong
new_note: Arunachal's hot-springs + dzong en route to Tawang — Dirang Dzong heritage stay + Pemaling Hotel. Organised Tawang-circuit operator format.

name: Diu
tagline: Portuguese colonial island — beaches, forts, and churches in one of India's hidden coastal gems
new_note: Former Portuguese enclave, prohibition-exempt so alcohol tourism — quieter than Goa, less harassment. Nagoa + Ghogla beaches are family-safe.

name: Doodhpathri
tagline: The Meadow of Milk — flat enough for a toddler to run on, stunning enough to make an adult cry.
new_note: Kashmir's quiet alt-meadow — day-trip from Srinagar, thin private infrastructure. Organised picnic-tour format only.

name: Drass
tagline: The second coldest inhabited place on Earth, a war memorial that will make you cry, and -45°C winters.
new_note: Kargil-Drass war-memorial town at 3,280m — JKTDC Tourist Bungalow + RTDC. Day-visit en route Srinagar-Leh; don't solo-overnight in the Drass strip.

name: Dudhsagar Falls
tagline: India's 5th tallest waterfall — 310 metres of white fury straddling the Goa-Karnataka border
new_note: Goa-Karnataka border waterfall — monsoon train or jeep safari only. Organised jeep from Mollem/Castle Rock is the safe format.

name: Dudhwa National Park
tagline: UP only national park — tigers, rhinos, and swamp deer in a terai wilderness that nobody from outside UP visits.
new_note: UP tiger reserve + Tharu tribal belt — UPTDC Dudhwa rest house is basic. Organised operator (Tiger Haven, Jaagir Lodge) recommended over self-book.

name: Dungarpur
tagline: The palace town south of Udaipur that even palace enthusiasts don't know about.
new_note: Mewar heritage, thin tourist flow — Udai Bilas Palace hotel is the only safe stay. Day-trip from Udaipur, don't rely on local transit.

name: Dwarka
tagline: Krishna's legendary kingdom — Char Dham and one of the 7 sacred cities of Hinduism
new_note: Jagat Mandir trust-run guesthouses prioritise women's safety. Beach is quiet + accessible. Dwarkadheesh evening aarti ends by 8pm, town shuts early.

name: Dzukou Valley
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) required for all Indian citizens (Nagaland entry). Apply online at eilp.nagaland.gov.in — free. Foreigners need RAP.
tagline: Valley of Flowers of the Northeast — rolling green meadows, rare Dzukou Lily, and soul-stirring silence.
new_note: Nagaland-Manipur trek + base camp — organised group (Zone Hotel, Kohima Tourism) is the safe format. Solo-women intra-valley not standard.

name: Elephanta Caves
tagline: UNESCO rock-cut caves on an island — a 6th-century Shiva masterpiece an hour from Mumbai
new_note: Boat trip from Gateway of Mumbai — tourists all day, last boat 5pm back. Caves are crowd-safe. Day-trip only, no overnight option.

name: Ellora Caves
tagline: UNESCO site with the monolithic Kailasa temple — carved top-down from a single basalt cliff
new_note: Aurangabad tourist circuit — ASI-managed, tourist police, women-run local guide cooperatives. Day-trip via MTDC bus from Aurangabad.

name: Eravikulam National Park
tagline: Tame Nilgiri Tahrs, South India's highest peak, and the 12-year flower bloom
new_note: Munnar-adjacent Nilgiri Tahr park — forest-dept-led bus entry only, no solo walking. Day-visit from Munnar is the standard safe format.

name: Fatehpur Sikri
tagline: Akbar built an entire capital city in 1571, used it for 14 years, then abandoned it forever — the red sandstone ghost city still stands untouched.
new_note: Mughal ghost-city + Sheikh Salim dargah. Authorised guides only — unauthorized guides + begging well-documented. Day-trip from Agra, back by 4pm.

name: Fort Aguada & Candolim
tagline: Portuguese stronghold turned luxury landmark — Goa's best-preserved fort meets premium beach
new_note: Fort + beach + Taj Fort Aguada — day visit is tourist-safe. Overnight at Taj property, not the Sinquerim beach shacks.

name: Gagron Fort
permit_required: none
tagline: India's only hill-and-water fort — UNESCO World Heritage
new_note: Rajasthan's Jal Durg at 3-river confluence — UNESCO, ASI-staffed day visit. Stay in Jhalawar town (RTDC Chandrawati), not at fort.

name: Gahirmatha
tagline: World's largest Olive Ridley turtle rookery — 600,000 turtles nest in one season
new_note: Olive ridley nesting (Jan-Mar) — forest-dept-regulated visits only, no solo-female infrastructure. Organised Dangmal/Gahirmatha tours only.

name: Gandhinagar
tagline: India's greenest capital — Akshardham, Adalaj Stepwell, wide boulevards lined with trees
new_note: Gujarat capital — Akshardham + Indroda park + planned-city wide roads. Alcohol-prohibition + metro under-construction = low street incident rate. Day-stop or overnight fine.

name: Gandikota
tagline: India's Grand Canyon — Pennar River gorge with a medieval fort on top
new_note: AP's Grand Canyon — daytime tour, APTDC cottage stay available. Remote enough that solo over-night iffy; organised tour from Hyderabad/Kurnool.

name: Gangotri
tagline: The origin of the Ganges — a temple at 3,100m where Goddess Ganga descended to Earth, surrounded by deodar forests and Himalayan peaks.

name: Gangtok
permit_required: Indian nationals: no permit for Gangtok. Foreigners: Restricted Area Permit from tourism office. Nathula Pass: separate permit required — Indians only, closed to foreigners.
tagline: India's cleanest hill town — monasteries, momos, and jaw-dropping Kanchenjunga views from a state where plastic bags have been illegal since 1998.
new_note: MG Marg is India's cleanest + most female-walkable hill town. No-vehicle zone + CCTV + local women's police. Stay in MG Marg area hotels.

name: Ganpatipule
tagline: Where the Ganesh temple meets pristine Konkan sand — pilgrimage and beach in one
new_note: Konkan Ganesh temple + beach; MTDC resort is the pick, self-contained + clean. Don't swim after dark (beach isn't guarded).

name: Gir National Park
tagline: Last refuge of the Asiatic lion — the only place on Earth to see them in the wild
new_note: Asiatic lion reserve — organised safari via Gir Sinh Sadan/Wildlife Division + fixed permits. Stay in Gir Sasan at GujTour resort. Safe, female-routine.

name: Mount Girnar
tagline: Sacred 10,000-step climb to 5 peaks — Jain and Hindu temples at 3,666 ft
new_note: Jain + Hindu pilgrim climb in Junagadh — 10,000-step trek, pilgrim-flow daytime. Don't climb at 3am solo; start at 5:30am when other women are on the trail.

name: Gokarna
tagline: What Goa was 30 years ago — Om Beach, temple town, and five beaches connected by cliff trails
new_note: Karnataka temple-town + 5-beach cliff-trek — female backpackers year-round. Om + Kudle are safer than the far-north Half Moon and its adjacent cove stretch after dark.

name: Gopalpur-on-Sea
tagline: Colonial-era seaside charm — lighthouse, quiet waves, and old-world peace
new_note: Odisha coastal town — OTDC Panthaniwas is safe, family beach. Avoid the stretch past the lighthouse after sunset.

name: Gopeshwar
tagline: Ancient Shiva temple + gateway to Chopta-Tungnath + the town where the Chipko movement began.
new_note: Chamoli district HQ — clean Garhwali town, pilgrim transit to Tungnath/Chopta. GMVN guesthouse safe; thin private options.

name: Great Himalayan National Park
permit_required: National Park entry permit required — arrange at Sairopa office, ₹200 Indian/₹400 foreigner
tagline: UNESCO World Heritage 2014 — 1,171 sq km of virgin Himalayan forest with snow leopard, brown bear, and 209 bird species.

name: Gulbarga (Kalaburagi)
tagline: Bahmani dynasty's first capital — India's only fully-roofed mosque and emerging Buddhist sites

name: Gulmarg
tagline: Asia's highest cable car, India's best skiing, and a meadow so green in summer that it hurts your eyes.
new_note: Kashmir ski-resort + gondola — organised package (J&K Tourism + Heevan Resort, Khyber) is the safe format. Solo-women routine on gondola + ski-school.

name: Guptkashi
tagline: The last comfortable town before Kedarnath — where smart pilgrims base themselves instead of suffering at 3583m.
new_note: Kedarnath base-town — GMVN + Char Dham Camp hotels are safe. Yatra-season (May-Oct) is the active safe window; winter dead.

name: Gurez Valley
tagline: The most beautiful valley you've never heard of — because it's on the LoC and was closed to civilians until 2007.
new_note: Kashmir LoC-adjacent permit zone — J&K Tourism Authority permits + military-checkpoint route. Organised operator (Mountainface, Gurez Lodge) is the only format.

name: Gurudongmar Lake
permit_required: Protected Area Permit (PAP) required — arranged through registered Sikkim tour operator. Indian citizens only.
tagline: Sacred lake at 17,800 ft — one of highest lakes in the world, frozen Nov-May, stunning turquoise in summer.
new_note: North Sikkim permit-only lake — same Lachen/Lachung package-tour context. Organised operator is the only female-safe way. Solo not permitted.

name: Guruvayur
tagline: Most sacred Krishna temple in Kerala — 60+ temple elephants

name: Guwahati
permit_required: No permit required for Indian or foreign nationals.
tagline: Gateway to the Northeast — ancient Kamakhya Temple, Brahmaputra river cruises, and the crossroads of seven sisters.
new_note: Gateway to the NE — Kamakhya pilgrims + tourists. Fancy Bazaar + Paltan Bazaar are safe; cross-river Umananda solo is iffy after dark.

name: Gwalior
permit_required: false
tagline: The pearl amongst fortresses
new_note: Fort + Jai Vilas Palace are tourist-safe; old city stretch at dusk feels heavier than expected. Stay near the fort, use pre-paid auto.

name: Haflong
tagline: Only hill station in Assam — Jatinga, the village where birds mysteriously crash-land at night during monsoon
new_note: Assam's only hill + Dimasa heritage — thin tourist flow, organised operator recommended. Day-trip from Silchar.

name: Halebidu
tagline: UNESCO Hoysala masterpiece — Hoysaleshwara Temple with 240 wall panels of mythological scenes
new_note: Hoysala trio tourist-safe — day tour with driver from Bangalore or Mysore covers it. ASI-managed, no dodgy areas. Skip the Belur-Halebidu bus if solo.

name: Hampi
tagline: UNESCO ruins of a forgotten empire — boulders, temples, and the ghost of Vijayanagara

name: Hanle
tagline: Darkest skies in India — a remote observatory village at 4500m where the Milky Way is visible to the naked eye.

name: Har Ki Doon
tagline: Cradle-shaped valley trek in the Govind sanctuary — the classic Garhwal walk.
new_note: Roadless Sankri-base trek — registered operators (Indiahikes, Trek The Himalayas) is the only safe format. No mobile, no stays beyond basecamp.

name: Haridwar
tagline: Where the Ganges leaves the mountains and enters the plains — evening Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri is India's most electric spiritual spectacle.
new_note: Ganga Aarti crowds are dense but safe — women-only queue at Har Ki Pauri, CCTV coverage. Har Ki Pauri hotels, not the lanes behind the station.

name: Harihareshwar
tagline: Deva che Ghar — where Shiva meets the sea, flanked by four protective hills
new_note: Konkan pilgrim + beach combo — MTDC resort is women-safe; Sagar Gunj homestays are family-run. Diveagar-Murud combo works.

name: Havelock Island (Swaraj Dweep)
permit_required: Indian nationals: none. Foreigners: 30-day permit on arrival at Port Blair airport (free)
tagline: Asia's best beach, world-class diving, and the island every Andaman trip revolves around
new_note: Andaman's main backpacker island — solo-female dive hubs (Barefoot, Dive India) normal. Radhanagar + Elephant Beach are patrolled. Monsoon closed.

name: Hemkund Sahib
tagline: Sikh pilgrimage shrine at 4,633m — a 6km walk up from Ghangaria, beside a glacial lake.
new_note: Gurdwara at 4,329m — yatra-season (Jul-Oct) has SGPC langar + organised shared-taxi from Govindghat. Group trek normal; solo rare but possible.

name: Hogenakkal Falls
tagline: India's Niagara — coracle rides through waterfalls and a 2,000-year-old oil massage tradition
new_note: Cauvery waterfalls day-trip — boat operators are organised, lifejackets mandatory. Don't swim, don't wander off the tourist loop.

name: Horsley Hills
tagline: British-era hill station at 1,265m — AP's quiet answer to Ooty
new_note: AP's budget hill near Madanapalle — APTDC Haritha resort is the safe base. Thin private infrastructure; day/overnight combine with Gandikota.

name: Hyderabad
tagline: Biryani capital of India — Charminar, Golconda, and 400 years of Nizam grandeur
new_note: HITEC City + Banjara Hills are night-safe; Old City is culturally conservative, less harassment but less transport. Metro is reliable, auto-rickshaw meter-mafia known.

name: Idukki
tagline: Asia's highest arch dam, cardamom hills, and Kerala's wildlife hub
new_note: Kerala's hill-dam district — sparse tourist flow, DTPC homestays + plantation bungalows. Day-trips from Munnar; don't solo-overnight in deep Idukki.

name: Igatpuri
tagline: Vipassana meditation headquarters, monsoon waterfalls, and Sahyadri treks
new_note: Vipassana centre + monsoon-Sahyadri — Dhamma Giri vipassana campus is female-safe (10-day silent retreat). Monsoon weekender stay is iffy.

name: Imphal
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) required for all Indian citizens. Apply online at manipurilp.nic.in — free, instant. Foreigners need Restricted Area Permit from MHA.
tagline: World's largest all-women market, ancient Kangla Fort, and the birthplace of polo.
new_note: Manipur capital — daytime tourist-safe (Ima Keithel all-women market is the highlight); curfew-era vigilance persists outskirts. Hotel Classic + CityRoom safe.

name: Jabalpur
tagline: Marble Rocks of Bhedaghat — 100-foot marble cliffs on the Narmada, Dhuandhar Falls, and gateway to Kanha and Bandhavgarh.
new_note: MP's Marble Rocks + Dhuandhar Falls + Bandhavgarh gateway — MPTDC Hotel Kalchuri + Hotel Narmada Jacksons. Bhedaghat day-trip; don't overnight at Bhedaghat.

name: Jagdalpur
permit_required: false
tagline: Chitrakote Falls and the Bastar tribal heartland
new_note: Chhattisgarh's Bastar HQ + Chitrakoot Falls — CTDC properties + organised tours only. Solo-women travel flow absent; skip.

name: Jaipur
tagline: Every fort in Jaipur makes you feel like a Maharaja, and the city dresses in pink to make sure you notice.
new_note: Pink City is tourist-safe daytime; cycle-rickshaw + Ola/Uber reliable. Avoid Ajmeri Gate stretch after 9pm. Chokhi Dhani resort-style dining is female-safe.

name: Jaisalmer
tagline: The only fort in India where 3000 people still LIVE — shops, temples, hotels, inside a 12th-century citadel.
new_note: Golden Fort guesthouses (Killa Bhawan, Helsinki House) are female-run. Sam-dune sunset trips must be with registered operators, not hotel-push touts.

name: Jibhi
tagline: The village that Instagram discovered — waterfalls, river, cafes, and the next Kasol but without the drugs.
new_note: Tirthan-adjacent Kullu valley — Jibhi Cafe + Artists Lodge + Chalet Silvan are women-owned. Tourist-safe emerging, still homestay-friendly.

name: Jodhpur
tagline: Mehrangarh is the fort that makes every other fort in Rajasthan feel like a rehearsal.
new_note: Blue City homestays near Mehrangarh are female-run (Cosy, RAAS, Pal Haveli). Stepwell area is daytime only. Overnight train to Jaipur runs on schedule, ladies coach recommended.

name: Jog Falls
tagline: India's second-highest plunge waterfall — 253 metres of monsoon fury in four cascades
new_note: Karnataka monsoon waterfall — KSTDC Mayura + Saguna Resort. Day-trip Mangalore-Shimoga; don't solo-overnight in falls-adjacent lodges.

name: Jorhat
tagline: Tea capital of the world — Gibbon Wildlife Sanctuary (India's only hoolock gibbon habitat), gateway to Majuli island
new_note: Assam's tea-HQ + Majuli ferry + Hollongapar gibbon-sanctuary — Banyan Grove + Heritage Chang Bungalow. Day-stop, not a destination.

name: Joshimath
tagline: One of Shankaracharya four mathas — and the town that is literally sinking since 2023.
new_note: Badrinath + Auli + VOF base — GMVN Joshimath + Mana Hotel. Subsidence crisis (2023) means check which hotels currently operational; don't self-drive unverified.

name: Junagadh
tagline: Ancient fortress city — Buddhist caves, Ashokan edicts, and Nawabi heritage at Girnar's foot

name: Kabini
tagline: India's most luxurious wildlife experience — Kabini backwaters, black panthers, and elephant herds
new_note: Orange County + Kabini River Lodge + JLR — all vetted, women-routine. Organised boat + jeep safari. Mysuru → 2.5hr drive.

name: Kadmat Island
permit_required: Entry permit required.
tagline: Water sports capital of Lakshadweep — best diving, kayaking, and the longest sandbank
new_note: Lakshadweep dive-focused island — SPORTS-run accommodation, mixed-gender dorms. Divers' community = female-safe.

name: Kalimpong
permit_required: false
tagline: Nursery capital of India
new_note: Bhutia-Lepcha-Nepali heritage hill near Darjeeling — Himalayan Hotel + Hilltop are safe. Durpin monastery + cactus nursery daytime. Quieter + safer than Darjeeling town.

name: Kalpa
tagline: Wake up to the Kinner Kailash massif from your guesthouse balcony — that view is the entire point.
new_note: Kinnaur's deodar-forest town — HPTDC Kinner Kailash + Kalpa Retreat are the picks. Organised tour from Shimla/Sarahan is the safe format.

name: Kanatal
tagline: North India glamping capital — apple orchards, stargazing, zero cell signal.
new_note: Orchard + camp hill station — glamping operators (Club Mahindra, Kanatal Villas) are resort-style, safe. Don't rely on unverified camps.

name: Kanchipuram
tagline: City of 1,000 Temples — sacred for both Shaivites and Vaishnavites, and home to India's finest silk
new_note: Temple town with organised tours from Chennai. Silk-weaving workshops are women-run + welcoming. Day-trip, don't overnight solo.

name: Kanha National Park
permit_required: none
tagline: Where Mowgli roamed — India's most famous tiger reserve
new_note: MPTDC Baghira + Van Vihar + Celebration Van Vilas are safe. Safari + naturalist-escorted. Mukki + Khatia gates tourist-regular.

name: Kannur
tagline: Theyyam ritual art, India's only drive-in beach, and Portuguese forts
new_note: Theyyam-ritual tourism + Muzhappilangad drive-in beach — daytime tour + guided Theyyam venues are the safest way. Local homestays in Parassinikadavu.

name: Kanyakumari
tagline: India's southernmost tip — the only place where you watch sunrise AND sunset over the sea
new_note: Sunset + sunrise tourism at India's tip — dense family crowd, tourist-bubble safe. Avoid Vivekananda Rock boat after sunset. Don't overnight in the dead town.

name: Karaikal
permit_required: No permit required.
tagline: Quiet Puducherry enclave on the Cauvery delta — ancient temples, French fort ruins, and pristine beach
new_note: Pondicherry-enclave Shiva temple circuit — quiet, safe, but thin infrastructure. Day-trip from Puducherry only.

name: Kargil
tagline: The gateway between Kashmir and Ladakh, a war-scarred town finding peace — and the start of the Zanskar road.
new_note: Ladakh-J&K border town — daytime overnight en route Srinagar-Leh. D'Zojila + The Kargil hotels. Don't walk the bazaar stretch at night.

name: Karwar
tagline: Tagore's favourite beach, India's naval base, and the gateway to Devbagh Island
new_note: Karnataka-Goa border port town — Navy base = police presence. Hotels on the strip, daytime beach only.

name: Kasauli
tagline: The closest proper hill station to Delhi/Chandigarh — and the army keeps it from turning into another Shimla.
new_note: Cantonment-quiet hill station — Army presence = low crime. Mall Road daytime walks, stay in Alasia or Kasauli Regency.

name: Kashid
tagline: Maharashtra's best white sand beach — clean, calm, and just close enough for a Mumbai weekend
new_note: Konkan weekend beach from Mumbai — crowded Sat-Sun with stag groups; quieter weekday. MTDC resort safer than private shacks.

name: Kasol
tagline: India's backpacker capital in the Parvati Valley — Israeli cafes, riverside camping, and mountain vibes.

name: Kausani
tagline: 300km of Himalayan panorama from your hotel balcony — if the clouds cooperate.
new_note: Himalayan-panorama hill town — KMVN Anashakti Ashram is female-safe. Avoid private lodges without bookings; tourist flow thinner than Nainital.

name: Kavaratti
permit_required: MANDATORY: Entry Permit from Lakshadweep Administration, Kochi. Indians: apply 2 weeks ahead. Foreigners: only Bangaram allowed.
tagline: Capital island of Lakshadweep — aquarium, mosques, and the calmest lagoon in India
new_note: Lakshadweep HQ island — entry permit required, women tourists routine on Samudram package. Lagoon resort-only overnight.

name: Kaza
tagline: The Spiti headquarters — every route in the valley runs through here.
new_note: Spiti Valley HQ at 3,800m — Spiti Holiday Adventure + Zostel are female-safe. Village homestays (Komic, Langza) vetted. Don't drive solo in winter.

name: Kaziranga
permit_required: No permit required. Park entry permit from forest office — ₹100 Indians, ₹650 foreigners.
tagline: One-horned rhino capital of the world — UNESCO site with the highest density of tigers in India.
new_note: Assam one-horned rhino reserve — elephant + jeep safari organised. Assam Tourism IORA + Diphlu River Lodge + Wild Grass safe. Solo-women routine.

name: Kedarnath
tagline: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas, sitting at 3583m beneath a glacier — open only 6 months a year.
new_note: Helicopter or trek — both heavily supervised. Yatra season (Apr-Nov) has 24/7 police + women's SHG food stalls. Winter is closed.

name: Keylong
tagline: Lahaul district HQ — the most important town nobody stops in, gateway to Leh from HP side.
new_note: Lahaul district HQ + Rohtang-north highway — HPTDC Chandrabhaga + New Hotel. Sep-Oct is the active window (before snow); thin otherwise.

name: Khajuraho
tagline: Erotic temple sculptures from 1,000 years ago that make you wonder what happened to India between then and now — UNESCO masterpieces in a sleepy village.

name: Khandala
tagline: The twin of Lonavala — Bollywood's favourite ghats, Duke's Nose, and the misty Western Ghats
new_note: Lonavala-adjacent — Duke's Nose viewpoint daytime. Same stag-group concerns. Stay in Lonavala, not roadside Khandala lodges.

name: Khangchendzonga National Park
permit_required: Kanchenjunga National Park permit required — arrange through registered Sikkim tour operator.
tagline: India's ONLY mixed UNESCO World Heritage Site — home to the world's third-highest peak at 8,586m. Sacred to the Lepcha people.
new_note: West Sikkim trek system — Dzongri + Goecha-la routes, permit + guide mandatory. Organised operator (Tashila, Sikkim Off The Beat) only.

name: Khardung La
tagline: 5,359m pass north of Leh — the gateway to Nubra Valley and a rite of passage for motorcyclists.
new_note: High-pass day-trip from Leh — Ladakh Autonomous Hill Council taxis + permit. Don't overnight at the pass. Solo-women OK on organised day tour.

name: Khonoma
permit_required: ILP (Inner Line Permit) required for Nagaland — apply online at ilp.nagaland.gov.in or at Dimapur/Kohima checkpost.
tagline: India's first self-declared green village — Angami Naga warriors who banned hunting and built a global conservation model.
new_note: Nagaland's green village + community-tourism model — Dovipie Inn + homestays (women-led conservation co-op). Safe in community-homestay format.

name: Kinnaur
tagline: The drive through Kinnaur is the destination — suicide roads, apple villages, and temples carved into cliffs.
new_note: Wide region — Kalpa + Reckong Peo are safe; Sangla/Chitkul need 4WD + guide. Go in July-September, skip winter.

name: Kishtwar
tagline: Where the Himalaya gets wild — Kishtwar is the adventure frontier that even Ladakh seekers skip.
new_note: Jammu's remote district + Hindu pilgrim route to Machail Mata — permit-zone history, insurgency-affected belt. Organised yatra-group only; skip solo.

name: Kochi (Fort Kochi)
tagline: Chinese Fishing Nets, oldest church in India, and Asia's largest art biennale

name: Kodaikanal
tagline: Princess of Hill Stations — star-shaped lake, Coaker's Walk cliff promenade, and the 12-year Kurinji bloom
new_note: Star-shaped lake + Coakers Walk = daytime-safe. Stay near Bryant Park, not the Observatory Road stretch. Russian Inn + KTDC are reliable.

name: Kohima
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) required for all Indian citizens. Apply online at eilp.nagaland.gov.in — free, issued within 24 hours. Foreigners need Restricted Area Permit (RAP) from MHA.
tagline: Naga warrior heritage, WWII cemetery, and the legendary Hornbill Festival — where 16 tribes celebrate as one.
new_note: Nagaland capital — Hornbill Festival (Dec) draws female solo travellers; Kohima Camp + Razhu Pru homestays are vetted. Off-festival, standard caution.

name: Kokernag
tagline: Kashmir's largest freshwater spring — trout hatchery, botanical garden, and water pure enough to drink from source.
new_note: Anantnag's freshwater spring + botanical garden — JKTDC Tourist Bungalow safe. Day-trip from Pahalgam; thin private solo-female infrastructure.

name: Kolad
tagline: Maharashtra's Rishikesh — white water rafting on the Kundalika River, 2 hours from Mumbai
new_note: Kundalika-river rafting from Mumbai/Pune — organised rafting operator (Vivanta, Suyash) is the safe format. Stag-weekender culture = don't walk strip alone.

name: Kolanupaka
tagline: 2,000-year-old jade Mahaveer statue — Telangana's hidden Jain treasure
new_note: Jain pilgrim site near Hyderabad — organised tours include it, but no solo traveller infrastructure exists. Day-trip via agency.

name: Kolhapur
tagline: Mahalaxmi Temple, kushti wrestling, and the spiciest misal pav you'll ever eat
new_note: Mahalaxmi temple + Shahu palace = Maharashtrian tourism, women-friendly family infrastructure. Avoid the stretch past New Palace after 9pm.

name: Kolkata
permit_required: false
tagline: India's cultural capital — where the trams still run, the Puja pandals rewrite themselves every October, and College Street's coffee-house conversations haven't ended since 1940.
new_note: Metro, yellow-taxi meter reliable, late-night trams in the central area. Park Street + Southern Avenue + Salt Lake are night-safe. Howrah is daytime-only for a first-time solo.

name: Kollam
tagline: Ashtamudi Lake, Munroe Island, and the 8-hour cruise to Alleppey
new_note: Backwater start-point — Alleppey boats are safe daytime, but Kollam town outside is thin. Day through-trip to Alleppey works better.

name: Konark
tagline: Sun Temple UNESCO — 24 stone wheels, 7 horses, and the birth of Odissi dance
new_note: Sun Temple tour from Puri — ASI-managed, daytime tour-safe. Don't overnight; Puri is the base. Nataraj Rath Yatra Dec is a crowd to plan around.

name: Konaseema
tagline: India's unknown backwaters — Godavari delta with 13 islands, zero tourists
new_note: AP's Godavari delta + coconut-grove — APTDC + Coringa organised tours. Homestays (Antarvedi, Dindi) family-run; Dindi Resorts is the female-safe pick.

name: Koraput
tagline: Dongria Kondh heartland — GI-tagged tribal textiles, Niyamgiri Hills, responsible tourism
new_note: Odisha tribal district — organised tour operators (Grass Routes) only. Solo traveller flow near-zero; infrastructure for that doesn't exist.

name: Kotagiri
tagline: The OLDEST Nilgiri hill station — Ooty without the crowds
new_note: Smallest Nilgiri town — Longwood Shola + tea estates. Thin stays; Kurumba Village + PRT Palace are the picks.

name: Kovalam
tagline: Kerala's most famous beach — lighthouse, Ayurveda, and the Arabian Sea
new_note: Lighthouse Beach strip is tourist-regular but male-harassment reports higher than Varkala. Hawa Beach end is quieter. Post-9pm stay in resort.

name: Kozhikode (Calicut)
tagline: Where Vasco da Gama landed — and Malabar biryani was perfected
new_note: Kerala's Malabar coast + literature festival town — family-tourist + student flow, Beach Road night-safe. Homestays (Harivihar) are female-run.

name: Kufri
tagline: Shimla snow playground — the reason half of North India drives to HP in winter.
new_note: Shimla's adventure-winter strip — yak + horse tourism, weekender crowd. Day-trip from Shimla only; thin hotels here.

name: Kukke Subramanya
tagline: Sarpa Dosha rituals in the Western Ghats — Subrahmanya temple and the Kumara Parvatha trailhead
new_note: Naga-dosha shrine popular with families — mixed pilgrim flow. Stay in the KSDT guesthouse, not the roadside lodges.

name: Kullu
tagline: The Valley of Gods — famous for Dussehra when 300+ village deities gather, plus rafting on the Beas and gateway to Manali.
new_note: Kullu-Manali valley town — pit-stop en route Manali. Stay in Manali, not Kullu. Shawl-weaver + Himachal Tourism day-stop format.

name: Kumarakom
tagline: Vembanad Lake — where Siberian birds winter and backwaters meet premium stays
new_note: Kerala backwater resort zone — CGH Coconut Lagoon + Kumarakom Lake Resort are gated + solo-women-routine. Houseboat via registered KTDC operator only.

name: Kumbakonam
tagline: Temple City — 188 temples, Airavatesvara (UNESCO), and the Mahamaham tank

name: Kumbalangi
tagline: India's first model ecotourism village — crab farming and Chinese fishing nets
new_note: Kerala's model tourism village — community-homestays (Kallancheri Retreat, Kumbalangi Homestay) women-run. Safe, friendly, Kochi-adjacent.

name: Kumbhalgarh
tagline: The second longest wall in the world after China — hilltop fort with 360-degree Aravalli views.
new_note: Rajasthan fort + leopard sanctuary — RTDC Hilltop + Aodhi are safe. Daytime fort + evening light show, organised safari. Solo overnight OK.

name: Kurukshetra
tagline: Where the Mahabharata war happened — or didn not — but the Brahma Sarovar and Krishna Museum make the mythology tangible.
new_note: Mahabharata-memorial town + Gita Gyan Sansthan — daytime Brahma Sarovar + museum. No overnight infrastructure for solo women; day-trip from Delhi/Ambala.

name: Kushinagar
tagline: Where the Buddha died — one of the four holiest Buddhist sites on Earth and India most undervisited pilgrimage.
new_note: Buddha's Parinirvana site + international Buddhist guesthouses (Thai, Japanese, Sri Lankan monasteries). Safe in their accommodation format; town infrastructure thin.

name: Kutch (Bhuj)
tagline: Gateway to the Rann — handicraft villages, Aina Mahal mirrors, and living tribal traditions
new_note: Bhuj + Rann Utsav (Nov-Feb) are overtly organised tourism — Dhordo tents are gated + vetted. Outside festival season, stay in Bhuj only.

name: Kuttanad
tagline: Below sea level farming — the only place in Asia where crops grow under water
new_note: Alleppey backwater villages — houseboat operators are regulated (Kerala Tourism register). Overnight houseboat solo is fine with a known operator.

name: Lachen
permit_required: Protected Area Permit required for North Sikkim.
tagline: North Sikkim base for Gurudongmar and Chopta Valley, Lachungpa tribal village.
new_note: North Sikkim permit zone — package-tour-only access (STDC-registered). Gurudongmar trip = organised vehicle + homestay. Solo infeasible.

name: Lachung
permit_required: North Sikkim Protected Area Permit (PAP) required for all — Indians and foreigners. Must be arranged through registered Sikkim travel agent in Gangtok. Cannot visit independently. Minimum 2 persons required.
tagline: Gateway to Yumthang Valley and Zero Point — rhododendron paradise in North Sikkim.
new_note: Yumthang Valley base — same package-tour structure as Lachen. Safe in organised format only.

name: Lahaul Valley
tagline: The valley beyond the Atal Tunnel — Keylong, Jispa, Darcha — where Himachal ends and Ladakh begins.
new_note: Himachal's cold-desert valley — Keylong base, organised Rohtang-Spiti circuit. Solo-women infrastructure thin; group-tour format only.

name: Laknavaram
tagline: 13 forested islands connected by a 160m suspension bridge — Kakatiya-era engineering
new_note: Telangana suspension-bridge lake — day-trip from Warangal, Telangana Tourism cottages. No solo overnight format.

name: Lamayuru
tagline: The moon landscape monastery — Ladakh at its most alien, with a 1000-year-old gompa perched above a crater of eroded clay.
new_note: Ladakh's moonscape + monastery — Lamayuru Guest House + Moonland Hotel. Day-stop en route Srinagar-Leh; organised overnight format fine.

name: Lambasingi
tagline: Only sub-zero place in South India — the Kashmir nobody knows about
new_note: AP's sub-zero hill — APTDC cottage + few homestays. Organised tour from Vizag is the female-safe format. Thin infrastructure.

name: Landour
tagline: The secret garden above Mussoorie that Ruskin Bond will never leave — Char Dukan, Lal Tibba, and walks with no one on them.
new_note: Mussoorie-adjacent cantonment-quiet heritage hill — Rokeby Manor + Doma's Inn + Char Dukan cluster. International solo-women regular.

name: Lansdowne
tagline: Army cantonment frozen in time — silent oak forests, no commercial circus.
new_note: Garhwal Rifles cantonment town — cleanest female-safe hill station in Uttarakhand. Army-run Fairy Dale + GMVN are trusted stays.

name: Leh
tagline: The town where every road trip to Ladakh begins with 2 mandatory days of doing nothing — and somehow those 2 days become the highlight.
new_note: Ladakh HQ — Main Bazaar + Changspa are night-safe; Zostel Plus + Hotel Grand Dragon + Omasila homestay family-run. Women's Alchi + Nimmu cooperative hosts solo.

name: Lepakshi
tagline: The hanging pillar that defies gravity — Vijayanagara's greatest artistic mystery
new_note: AP hanging-pillar temple day-trip from Bangalore — daytime tour-safe. Bus/taxi + tour operator standard. No overnight in the village.

name: Likir
tagline: A living monastery where monks still debate, pray, and tend gardens — plus a giant golden Maitreya Buddha visible for miles.
new_note: Ladakh monastery village near Leh — Nimmu House + Likir monastery guest-house. Day-trip or single overnight format. Organised Leh operator base.

name: Little Andaman
permit_required: Indian nationals: none. Foreigners: need special permit from DC office
tagline: Only surfing destination in Andaman — Butler Bay waves, White Surf Waterfall, and total isolation
new_note: Hut Bay + Butler Bay beach — remote, ferry-dependent, thin tourist flow. Organised tour via A&N Tourism is the only female-safe format.

name: Loktak Lake
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) required for all Indian citizens (Manipur entry). Apply online at manipurilp.nic.in — free.
tagline: Floating islands on a lake — phumdis, the last dancing deer, and a landscape found nowhere else on Earth.
new_note: Manipur floating-phumdi lake — Sendra Park & Resort is the safe stay. Boat to Keibul Lamjao NP organised. Day-trip from Imphal, don't solo-village overnight.

name: Lolab Valley
tagline: Kashmir hidden meadow valley — walnut trees, no tourists, and the silence that Gulmarg lost to gondola crowds.
new_note: Kashmir Kupwara-district valley — permit-zone, organised JK-Tourism tour only. Solo not feasible for women.

name: Lonar Crater
tagline: The world's only meteoric crater in basaltic rock — 50,000 years old, saline and alkaline
new_note: Maharashtra's meteorite-crater lake — MTDC Hotel Plaza + Lonar Tourist Lodge. Daytime crater-rim walk safe; don't venture off-trail solo.

name: Lonavala
tagline: Mumbai-Pune's favourite monsoon escape — fudge, fog, and fort trails
new_note: Pune-Mumbai weekenders make it loud + stag-group heavy. Camellia + Hilton Shillim outside town are safer than Mall Road hotels.

name: Long Island
permit_required: Indian nationals: none. Foreigners: need permit
tagline: Robinson Crusoe vibes — pristine Lalaji Bay beach with barely 50 tourists a month
new_note: Homestay-only remote Andaman island — Emerald Gecko is the main female-safe stay. Book ahead, plan full 2-night — ferry schedules unforgiving.

name: Lothal
tagline: World's oldest known dock — 4,500-year-old Indus Valley port city
new_note: Gujarat's Harappan port — ASI-staffed day-visit, Maritime Museum under renovation. Day-trip from Ahmedabad only.

name: Lucknow
tagline: The city where food is religion, manners are art, and the Bara Imambara is the most underrated monument in India.

name: Lunglei
permit_required: ILP required.
tagline: Second-largest town in Mizoram — colonial-era Lunglei Fort, Saikuti Hall, blue mountain views, gateway to Saiha and Phawngpui
new_note: Mizoram's second-largest town — thin tourist infrastructure, organised Mizoram Tourism tour only. Skip solo.

name: Machilipatnam
tagline: India's Kalamkari capital — hand-painted textiles since the 1600s
new_note: AP coastal town + kalamkari — thin tourist infrastructure. Day-trip from Vijayawada, cultural-cotton-printing workshops are female-run.

name: Madurai
tagline: The Lotus City — Meenakshi Temple with 33,000 sculptures and a midnight closing ritual
new_note: Meenakshi temple is women-safe; the stretch from Periyar bus stand to the temple has documented harassment. Stay inside the temple-access lane (Town Hall Road).

name: Mahabaleshwar
tagline: Strawberry capital of India — colonial viewpoints over the Krishna Valley
new_note: Pune-Mumbai weekend hill — family-tourist heavy. Mahabaleshwar Club + MTDC + Le Meridien are safe. Avoid the Panchgani road alone after dark.

name: Mahabalipuram (Mamallapuram)
tagline: UNESCO Pallava rock-cut temples on the Coromandel Coast — beach + heritage in one
new_note: Shore Temple + stone-carving village — UNESCO complex, tourist regular. Ideal Beach Resort is the pick. Beach swimming iffy in post-monsoon.

name: Majuli
permit_required: No permit required for Indian or foreign nationals.
tagline: World's largest inhabited river island — Vaishnavite monasteries, mask-making, and a culture fighting the Brahmaputra.
new_note: World's largest river island + Vaishnavite satras — ferry from Jorhat, community-homestays at Kamalabari (Mishing family-run) are female-safe.

name: Malvan
tagline: Scuba diving capital of the Konkan — Sindhudurg Fort, Malvani cuisine, and India's west coast reef
new_note: Konkan scuba base — resort-stay only. Daytime snorkel + Sindhudurg Fort day-trip; don't walk the Malvan beach strip at night.

name: Manali
tagline: The Goa of the mountains — overrun, overbuilt, and still useful as a launchpad for everything north of it.

name: Manas National Park
tagline: UNESCO World Heritage Project Tiger reserve — one-horned rhino, pygmy hog (world's smallest wild pig), golden langur
new_note: Assam's remote wildlife reserve — Bhutan-border, thinner infrastructure than Kaziranga. Organised tour via Kohora + Bansbari-registered lodges only.

name: Mandi
tagline: The gateway to Kullu Valley and beyond — 81 temples in one town and nobody stops to see them.
new_note: Mandi town + nearby Rewalsar are quiet, culturally conservative, solo-female-tolerant. HRTC bus safer than private. Skip the Paddal ground area at night.

name: Mandrem
tagline: Goa's wellness beach — yoga retreats, kitesurfing, and the quietest sand in North Goa

name: Mandu
permit_required: false
tagline: Romantic medieval citadel at 634m — Jahaz Mahal, Rani Roopmati's love story, and 45 sq km of Afghan-Mughal ruins that most of India has forgotten.
new_note: MP tourism town, Jahaz Mahal + Rani Roopmati pavilion day-tour from Indore. Stay at MPTDC Malwa Resort, not the private lodges. Monsoon (Aug-Sep) is the best window.

name: Mandvi
tagline: Beach town with 400-year-old palace — where dhows are still hand-built on the shore
new_note: Kutch coastal town + ship-building heritage — day-trip via Rann Utsav circuit. No solo overnight, infrastructure thin.

name: Mangalore
tagline: Coastal Karnataka's capital — Mangalorean cuisine, Kudla culture, and gateway to the Western Ghats

name: Manikaran
tagline: Natural hot springs where you can boil rice in the ground — plus a massive Sikh gurudwara and Shiva temple side by side.
new_note: Parvati-valley Sikh-Hindu hot-springs — gurdwara langar + overnight accommodation (gender-separated). Avoid the lodge strip at night; stay in gurdwara.

name: Marari Beach
tagline: Kerala's most pristine beach — fishing village life with no jet skis
new_note: Marari Beach resort zone between Alleppey and Kochi — gated, vetted, solo-female routine. Marari Beach Resort + Abad are the pick.

name: Maredumilli
tagline: Tribal eco-tourism hub — community-owned forests, bamboo chicken, and waterfalls
new_note: AP's Papikonda range tribal tourism — APTDC Jungle Star cottages + organised tribal tour. Solo-women flow near-zero.

name: Margao
tagline: South Goa's real capital — Konkani markets, Portuguese mansions, and the food Goans actually eat
new_note: South Goa's commercial HQ — transit waypoint, not a destination. Stay at Colva/Palolem instead. Day-stop for market + train station.

name: Marine National Park
tagline: India's first marine park — walk on coral reefs at low tide and see octopus, pufferfish
new_note: Gujarat's Jamnagar coral reef — permit-regulated, organised tour only. Day-visit from Jamnagar, no solo-female overnight.

name: Matheran
tagline: Asia's only automobile-free hill station — no engines, just birdsong and horse hooves
new_note: Car-free hill station near Mumbai — horse + hand-rickshaw transit only. MTDC Holiday Camp + Regal are safe, day-trip doable.

name: Mathura
tagline: Krishna's birthplace — the jail cell temple, Vishram Ghat on the Yamuna, and India's most colorful Holi.
new_note: Krishna-janmabhoomi area has police cover; surrounding lanes are crowded and eve-teasing documented. Day-trip from Agra or Delhi, don't overnight solo.

name: Mawlynnong
permit_required: No permit required for Indian or foreign nationals.
tagline: Asia's cleanest village — bamboo dustbins, living root bridges, and a community that shames cities.
new_note: Asia's 'cleanest village' in Meghalaya — all-female village council runs the bamboo homestays. Day-trip/overnight from Shillong, utterly female-safe.

name: Mawphlang
tagline: Sacred Forest of the Khasi tribe — 1,000-year-old untouched grove where nothing can be removed, not even a leaf.
new_note: Meghalaya's sacred grove + Khasi-heritage village — organised day-tour from Shillong, community guides + women-run Mawphlang Homestay. Female-safe.

name: Mawsynram
permit_required: No permit required for Indian or foreign nationals.
tagline: The actual wettest place on Earth — where umbrellas are a way of life and every surface drips green.
new_note: Wettest place on earth + cave access — Cherrapunji-adjacent organised tours cover it. Don't overnight solo; day-trip via Shillong operator.

name: McLeod Ganj
tagline: Home of the Dalai Lama — Tibetan cafes, meditation retreats, and the Triund trek start point at 2082m.
new_note: Tibetan-exile town = international female travellers year-round. Bhagsu + Dharamkot are female-backpacker hubs. Triund trek has guided groups daily.

name: Mechuka
permit_required: Inner Line Permit (ILP) MANDATORY for all Indian citizens. Apply online via Arunachal Pradesh eILP portal or at Guwahati/Tezpur facilitation centers.
tagline: Last airstrip before the China border with Siang River valley and tribal Memba culture
new_note: West Siang border-valley — PAP + organised tour (Abor Country) mandatory. Mechuka Homestay (Yargep Yomgam) is female-safe in guided format.

name: Medak
tagline: One of Asia's largest churches + a 12th-century fort — Deccan's hidden heritage
new_note: Telangana church town + fort — daytime tour from Hyderabad, no solo overnight. Safe in organised group context only.

name: Meghamalai (High Wavy Mountains)
permit_required: Estate permission + Wildlife Sanctuary permit
tagline: Cloud forest with zero tourism — advance estate booking required
new_note: TN's offbeat hill + tea estate — thin stays (Elephant Valley + High Wavys) + limited transport. Solo drive needed; sparse safety net.

name: Minicoy Island
permit_required: Entry permit required. Very limited tourist slots.
tagline: Southernmost island with a 300-year-old lighthouse, Maldivian culture, and tuna fishing heritage

name: Modhera Sun Temple
tagline: Exquisitely carved 11th-century Sun Temple — sun illuminates the inner sanctum at equinox
new_note: Sun Temple + step-well day-trip from Ahmedabad. Well-lit, ASI-maintained, tourist police during festival days. Don't overnight — town has nothing.

name: Mokokchung
permit_required: ILP required.
tagline: Heartland of the Ao Naga tribe — Longkhum village viewpoint, Ungma (oldest Ao village), Moatsu festival in May
